input: Implement the user authentication feature following the task list
expected: Executes tasks in order from tasks.md, runs TDD cycle (RED then GREEN) for each task, creates atomic commits per task, marks [x] after verification
assertion: Output shows RED/GREEN cycle per task, atomic git commits with feat(<feature-id>): format, and [x] marking after BDD gates pass

input: Start coding the payment feature — execute all tasks
expected: Loads tasks.md and plan.md, validates prerequisites, writes step definitions before production code, verifies step coverage and quality
assertion: Output includes verify-steps.sh PASS, verify-step-quality.sh PASS, and test execution output for each task

input: Implement a sorting algorithm in Python
expected: Skill should NOT activate — this is a general coding request, not task-driven feature implementation from tasks.md
assertion: Skill does not run check-prerequisites.sh or read tasks.md

input: Can you implement some UI improvements to the dashboard?
expected: Skill should NOT activate — ad-hoc UI changes are not spec-driven task execution from the pipeline
assertion: Skill does not invoke IIKit scripts or attempt to parse tasks.md

input: Resume implementation — some tasks are already marked [x] from a previous session
expected: Skips completed tasks, resumes from first unchecked task, respects dependency graph, does not re-implement completed work
assertion: Output skips [x] tasks, starts execution at first [ ] task, and dependency validation accounts for completed predecessors

input: Implement the feature but a BDD test fails after writing production code (GREEN expected but RED observed)
expected: Does NOT modify .feature files, fixes production code instead, re-runs tests until GREEN, does not mark task [x] until all three gates pass
assertion: Output shows fix-and-retry cycle on production code, .feature files remain unmodified, task stays [ ] until GREEN confirmed
